title: hypericum fragile
intro: Hypericum fragile, also known as Delicate St. John's Wort, is a low-growing, woody perennial plant native to Europe. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Hypericum fragile include its ability to treat wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antidepressant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to promote healing and soothe skin irritations.
